duplicate_sources.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025) (1).pdf
duplicate_sources.duplicate_of: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Require post-bronchodilator spirometry criteria for COPD diagnosis
rules.message: COPD diagnosis requires relevant symptoms and risk factors plus post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC below 0.7.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Avoid duplicate antimuscarinic inhalers in COPD
rules.message: Do not duplicate inhalers from the same class or combine SAMA and LAMA therapy because antimuscarinic adverse effects can increase.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Strongly encourage smoking cessation in COPD

question_relaxed_any: (?=.*(?:diagnosed with (?:COPD|chronic obstructive pulmonary disease)|I have (?:COPD|chronic obstructive pulmonary disease)|my (?:COPD|chronic obstructive pulmonary disease)))(?=.*(?:lifestyle|manage|living with|moving forward|what kind of changes|what changes))

rules.message: Smoking cessation is the most effective intervention for slowing COPD progression and should be strongly encouraged.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: The index of suspicion for COPD should be raised when the patient presents with chronic unexplained dyspnoea o
rules.message: The index of suspicion for COPD should be raised when the patient presents with chronic unexplained dyspnoea or reduced effort tolerance that tends to worsen over time, chronic cough with or without sputum production, or inspiratory/expiratory wheezing.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Start bronchodilator treatment depending on symptoms and risk of exacerbations (refer to Figure 4)
rules.message: Start bronchodilator treatment depending on symptoms and risk of exacerbations (refer to Figure 4).

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Due to similarities in symptoms, these differential diagnoses should be considered when assessing a patient pr
rules.message: Due to similarities in symptoms, these differential diagnoses should be considered when assessing a patient presenting with symptoms and risk factors suggestive of COPD.¹.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Symptoms assessment should be conducted at least yearly, and more frequently for patients who are more symptom
rules.message: Symptoms assessment should be conducted at least yearly, and more frequently for patients who are more symptomatic, have more frequent exacerbations, or have recent escalation in treatment.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: A COPD exacerbation is defined as an event characterised by dyspnoea and/or cough and sputum that worsens in <
rules.message: A COPD exacerbation is defined as an event characterised by dyspnoea and/or cough and sputum that worsens in <14 days which may be accompanied by tachypnoea and/or tachycardia and is often associated with increased local and systemic inflammation caused by infection, pollution, or other insults to the airways.9 Differential diagnoses that may present similarly should be excluded, such as pneumonia, congestive heart failure, or pulmonary embolism.1.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Treatment of COPD acute exacerbations should be initiated with short-acting inhaled beta2-agonists with or wit
rules.message: Treatment of COPD acute exacerbations should be initiated with short-acting inhaled beta2-agonists with or without antimuscarinics.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Patients who should be considered for treatment in the tertiary setting include those with severe signs and sy
rules.message: Patients who should be considered for treatment in the tertiary setting include those with severe signs and symptoms, unstable vitals (for example, respiratory rate ≥24 breaths/minute, heart rate ≥95 beats/minute, resting oxygen saturation <92% on room air and/or change >3% [when known]), serious comorbidities, or those who fail to respond to initial treatment.1.

source.document: Singapore Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease diagnosis and management (Dec 2024).pdf

rules.title: Do not routinely use benzodiazepines first-line for GAD
rules.message: Benzodiazepines should not routinely be prescribed as first-line treatment for GAD.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Continue SSRI or SNRI treatment after GAD remission

required_any: at least (?:another )?six months|at least (?:another )?6 months|6(?:–|-)12 months|six(?:–|-)twelve months

rules.message: After GAD remission on an SSRI or SNRI, continue the same dose for at least six months to reduce relapse risk.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Offer combined CBT and SSRI or SNRI for severe GAD
rules.message: For severe GAD, offer combined CBT-based psychological treatment and an SSRI or SNRI as first-line treatment when feasible and acceptable.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: For details on diagnostic assessment of GAD, clinicians may refer to the latest Diagnostic and Statistical Man
rules.message: For details on diagnostic assessment of GAD, clinicians may refer to the latest Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders or the International Classification of Diseases.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Clinicians should assess whether a patient’s condition is mild, moderate, or severe, based on the intensity of
rules.message: Clinicians should assess whether a patient’s condition is mild, moderate, or severe, based on the intensity of symptoms and the impact on subjective distress and daily functioning.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: First-line treatment options for GAD overlap with MDD and other anxiety disorders
rules.message: First-line treatment options for GAD overlap with MDD and other anxiety disorders.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Due to age-related changes, disability, and medical comorbidities, the balance of benefits and risks of GAD tr
rules.message: Due to age-related changes, disability, and medical comorbidities, the balance of benefits and risks of GAD treatments in older adults should be assessed individually.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to the upcoming Practice Guide for Tiered Care Model for Mental Health (Adult) for further details on su
rules.message: Refer to the upcoming Practice Guide for Tiered Care Model for Mental Health (Adult) for further details on suicide risk management and red flag situations.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to social services for financial assistance, social support, unemployment support, housing issues, and o
rules.message: Refer to social services for financial assistance, social support, unemployment support, housing issues, and other stressors, depending on need.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Though commonly referred to as ‘antidepressants’, selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) and serotoni
rules.message: Though commonly referred to as ‘antidepressants’, selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs) and serotonin–noradrenaline reuptake inhibitors (SNRIs) have anxiolytic effects, and are established first-line medications for GAD.

source.document: Singapore Generalised anxiety disorder easing burden and enabling remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Avoid routine statin use during pregnancy
rules.message: The guideline advises avoiding statins in pregnancy, breastfeeding, or while planning conception, with specialist referral for high-risk patients.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf
source.recommendation: Exercise care with statins in certain populations

rules.title: Consider statin treatment for intermediate cardiovascular risk
rules.message: For a 10-year cardiovascular risk of 10% to 20%, consider a moderate-intensity statin, especially when risk enhancers are present.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Use intensive lipid lowering for established cardiovascular disease

question_all: cardiovascular disease|coronary disease|heart attack|myocardial infarction|stroke|peripheral artery disease|ASCVD

rules.message: Patients with established atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease should receive intensive lipid-lowering therapy using a maximally tolerated statin, adding ezetimibe when needed.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: While all patients should be reassessed and provided tailored advice on optimising lifestyle, some patients ma
rules.message: While all patients should be reassessed and provided tailored advice on optimising lifestyle, some patients may benefit from a personalised lifestyle management plan that specifies the quantity, timing, and type of action to drive behavioural change (see Recommendation 3).

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Avoid use of statin in women who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or planning to conceive, and refer to specialist
rules.message: Avoid use of statin in women who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or planning to conceive, and refer to specialist especially if at high risk.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Management should be tailored to individual goals and characteristics, including comorbidities
rules.message: Management should be tailored to individual goals and characteristics, including comorbidities.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Lifestyle interventions should be tailored based on readiness to change and individual context (such as occupa
rules.message: Lifestyle interventions should be tailored based on readiness to change and individual context (such as occupational and social support).

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: While older age (>75 years) is not a contraindication for statin use, factors to be considered include potenti
rules.message: While older age (>75 years) is not a contraindication for statin use, factors to be considered include potential benefits, risk of side effects (noting that renal or hepatic impairment and potential for drug interactions may be more likely), patient preference, and functional status.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: In pregnant women, choice of lipid-lowering therapy is limited by potential safety concerns, and balance of be
rules.message: In pregnant women, choice of lipid-lowering therapy is limited by potential safety concerns, and balance of benefits and risks should be individualised.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Lifestyle intervention, as a component of both primary and secondary prevention, should be tailored according
rules.message: Lifestyle intervention, as a component of both primary and secondary prevention, should be tailored according to the individual’s context, overall CV risk, and lipid profile.

source.document: Singapore Lipid management- focus on cardiovascular risk ACG (Dec 2023) v1.1.pdf

rules.title: Do not abruptly stop antidepressant treatment
rules.message: Antidepressants should be tapered gradually rather than stopped abruptly to reduce discontinuation symptoms.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Continue antidepressants for at least six months after MDD remission
rules.message: Continue an antidepressant at the optimal dose for at least six months after MDD remission to reduce relapse risk.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Assess causes before changing treatment for suboptimal MDD response

required_any: adherence|taking (?:it|the medication) as prescribed|dose|duration|diagnos|comorbid|adverse effect|side effect|possible reasons|review

rules.message: When initial MDD treatment response is suboptimal, assess possible reasons such as adherence, dose, duration, diagnosis, comorbidities, and adverse effects before changing strategy.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Combining a second-generation antidepressant with psychotherapy, or psychotherapy alone, are preferred for tre
rules.message: Combining a second-generation antidepressant with psychotherapy, or psychotherapy alone, are preferred for treating moderately severe and severe MDD, given greater efficacy and more favourable benefit-risk balance respectively (Recommendation 4a).

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Consider a safety plan for patients assessed to be at risk of suicide
rules.message: Consider a safety plan for patients assessed to be at risk of suicide.10 Further details on red flags for urgent attention, suicide risk assessment, developing a safety plan and referral pathways may be found in the upcoming Practice Guide for Tiered Care Model for Mental Health (Adult).

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Referral may be considered for patients with severe symptoms or severe functional impairment, as required
rules.message: Referral may be considered for patients with severe symptoms or severe functional impairment, as required.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: First-generation antidepressants refer to tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) and monoamine oxidase inhibitors (M
rules.message: First-generation antidepressants refer to tricyclic antidepressants (TCAs) and monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOIs), while second-generation antidepressants include selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs), serotonin-norepinephrine reuptake inhibitors (SNRIs), and other newer agents.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Second-generation antidepressants are recommended as the first-line antidepressants for treatment of MDD
rules.message: Second-generation antidepressants are recommended as the first-line antidepressants for treatment of MDD.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to Table 1 for key considerations to guide selection of an antidepressant for MDD treatment
rules.message: Refer to Table 1 for key considerations to guide selection of an antidepressant for MDD treatment.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: The importance of treatment adherence: Emphasise that if the patient decides to discontinue antidepressant tre
rules.message: The importance of treatment adherence: Emphasise that if the patient decides to discontinue antidepressant treatment, these medications should not be abruptly stopped but rather tapered off gradually.18,44 This is to minimise the development of discontinuation symptoms.17,19,§.

source.document: Singapore Major depressive disorder achieving and sustaining remission (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer recent-stent patients with new atrial fibrillation for antithrombotic review
rules.message: New-onset AF with a coronary stent placed within 12 months requires cardiology review to reassess antithrombotic therapy.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Do not combine nitrates with PDE-5 inhibitors
rules.message: Do not use nitrates concurrently with PDE-5 inhibitors such as sildenafil because severe hypotension can occur.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Confirm dual antiplatelet treatment duration after PCI

required_any: specified duration|treatment duration|cardiolog|referring (?:doctor|specialist)|do not stop|should not stop|confirm|check

rules.message: After PCI, confirm that dual antiplatelet therapy has a specified duration and check with the referring cardiologist if the duration is unclear.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Premature withdrawal of clopidogrel in patients with recent drug-eluting stent implantation (within past 1–3 m
rules.message: Premature withdrawal of clopidogrel in patients with recent drug-eluting stent implantation (within past 1–3 months) may be associated with stent thrombosis and potentially fatal consequences;13, 14 seek advice from the cardiologist if non-urgent surgery or diagnostic procedure is required for these patients.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Use the modified CHA2DS2VASc score (see Box 1) to guide the decision to initiate OAC therapy in patients with
rules.message: Use the modified CHA2DS2VASc score (see Box 1) to guide the decision to initiate OAC therapy in patients with CCS who do not have a mechanical heart valve or moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to the ACG Type 2 diabetes mellitus — personalising management with non-insulin medications
rules.message: Refer to the ACG Type 2 diabetes mellitus — personalising management with non-insulin medications.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Use an ACE inhibitor, ARB or calcium channel blocker as first-line antihypertensive medications
rules.message: Use an ACE inhibitor, ARB or calcium channel blocker as first-line antihypertensive medications; consider thiazide/thiazide-like diuretics as alternative first line if indicated.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to the ACG Hypertension — tailoring the management plan to optimise blood pressure control
rules.message: Refer to the ACG Hypertension — tailoring the management plan to optimise blood pressure control.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Refer to the ACG Chronic kidney disease — delaying progression and reducing cardiovascular complications
rules.message: Refer to the ACG Chronic kidney disease — delaying progression and reducing cardiovascular complications.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Use a CCB when beta blockers are contraindicated or cause unacceptable side effects
rules.message: Use a CCB when beta blockers are contraindicated or cause unacceptable side effects.

source.document: Singapore Management of chronic coronary syndrome (Mar 2025).pdf

rules.title: Offer a topical NSAID before routine oral NSAID treatment for knee osteoarthritis
rules.message: The guideline offers a topical NSAID and reserves short oral NSAID courses for cases where topical treatment is ineffective or unsuitable.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Do not inject intra-articular corticosteroid when infection is suspected
rules.message: Do not administer an intra-articular corticosteroid injection when joint infection is suspected.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Use education, exercise, and weight management as knee OA mainstays
rules.message: Patient education, exercise programmes, and weight management are the mainstay strategies for knee osteoarthritis and should be tailored to the patient.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: For patients with knee OA whose persistent symptoms significantly impact function and quality of life, discuss
rules.message: For patients with knee OA whose persistent symptoms significantly impact function and quality of life, discuss referral options for surgical or advanced pain management.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Patients with knee OA often present with debilitating symptoms, most commonly joint pain, alongside functional
rules.message: Patients with knee OA often present with debilitating symptoms, most commonly joint pain, alongside functional limitations.10 While pain and function are two primary assessment domains for knee OA, an effective biopsychosocial assessment should also include quality of life and psychosocial factors (Figure 2).

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: The analgesic effects of oral NSAIDs can begin within two hours of starting treatment and peak after two weeks
rules.message: The analgesic effects of oral NSAIDs can begin within two hours of starting treatment and peak after two weeks, but diminish after eight weeks.59 Given that adverse effects may appear after four weeks of treatment,59 NSAIDs should be used only when needed for the shortest possible duration and at the lowest effective dose.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: While paracetamol is generally considered safe and serves as the first-line analgesic in the pain management l
rules.message: While paracetamol is generally considered safe and serves as the first-line analgesic in the pain management ladder, its effectiveness in knee OA is limited.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Depending on patient needs and risk factors, clinicians can refer patients to other allied health professional
rules.message: Depending on patient needs and risk factors, clinicians can refer patients to other allied health professionals.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Patients who require additional support and nutritional guidance for weight management can benefit from referr
rules.message: Patients who require additional support and nutritional guidance for weight management can benefit from referral to dietitians, especially if they have obesity, concurrent metabolic disease or those with multiple unsuccessful weight loss attempts.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Patients with psychosocial risk factors can benefit from referral to psychologists, for interventions such as
rules.message: Patients with psychosocial risk factors can benefit from referral to psychologists, for interventions such as cognitive behavioural therapy to provide additional improvements in pain, function, insomnia and depression.70,71 For guidelines on management of mental health conditions in primary care, refer to the ACGs on major depressive disorder and generalised anxiety disorder.

source.document: Singapore Management of knee osteoarthritis a joint effort with patients ACG (Apr 2026).pdf

rules.title: Avoid triple corticosteroid-antibiotic-antifungal products for infected atopic dermatitis
rules.message: Avoid triple products combining a topical corticosteroid, antibiotic, and antifungal for suspected or evident bacterial infection in atopic dermatitis.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Do not routinely use oral corticosteroids for atopic dermatitis
rules.message: Oral corticosteroids should not be used routinely for atopic dermatitis; the guideline limits them to selected short-course situations.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Continue topical anti-inflammatory treatment during infected AD
rules.message: During secondary infection of atopic dermatitis, continue the patient's topical anti-inflammatory treatment alongside appropriate antimicrobial treatment.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Long-term oral corticosteroids should not be used due to increased complications including gestational diabete
rules.message: Long-term oral corticosteroids should not be used due to increased complications including gestational diabetes.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Manage secondary bacterial infections
rules.message: Manage secondary bacterial infections; refer severe complications such as severe cellulitis and eczema herpeticum urgently.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Avoid triple combination products of TCS, antibiotics and antifungals in AD patients with suspected or clinica
rules.message: Avoid triple combination products of TCS, antibiotics and antifungals in AD patients with suspected or clinically evident bacterial infection.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: If a non-steroidal alternative is required or preferred, consider topical calcineurin inhibitors (as second-li
rules.message: If a non-steroidal alternative is required or preferred, consider topical calcineurin inhibitors (as second-line), or topical phosphodiesterase 4 inhibitors (as third-line).

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Do not routinely prescribe oral corticosteroids for AD, except as a short course for rescue therapy or bridgin
rules.message: Do not routinely prescribe oral corticosteroids for AD, except as a short course for rescue therapy or bridging therapy to systemic treatment.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Prescribe topical corticosteroids as first-line anti-inflammatory treatment for active lesions
rules.message: Prescribe topical corticosteroids as first-line anti-inflammatory treatment for active lesions.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: While disease severity, patient age and anatomical site (Table 3) are useful for guiding treatment selection,

question_relaxed_any: (?=.*(?:eczema|atopic dermatitis|\bAD\b))(?=.*(?:reassess|reassessment|severity|treatment is still right|treatment still right|better treatment|different treatment|change over time))

rules.message: While disease severity, patient age and anatomical site (Table 3) are useful for guiding treatment selection, clinicians should also consider other patient factors, treatment history, cost and availability.

source.document: Singapore Mild and moderate atopic dermatitis eczema  a journey from flare to care Feb 2026.pdf

rules.title: Do not routinely perform dengue PCR after day seven
rules.message: Dengue PCR generally should not be performed more than seven days after symptom onset.

rules.title: Avoid NSAIDs in suspected or confirmed dengue
rules.message: Avoid NSAIDs and aspirin in dengue because of bleeding risk; paracetamol may be considered when liver dysfunction is not suspected.

rules.title: Do not transfuse platelets prophylactically in stable non-bleeding dengue
rules.message: A stable dengue patient who is not bleeding and is not undergoing a procedure does not require prophylactic platelet transfusion regardless of platelet count.

rules.title: For the diagnosis of dengue infection, symptoms, signs and laboratory test results should be consistent with d
rules.message: For the diagnosis of dengue infection, symptoms, signs and laboratory test results should be consistent with dengue infection.

rules.title: In the first 7 days from symptom onset of suspected dengue infection, tests to detect both dengue virus NS1 an
rules.message: In the first 7 days from symptom onset of suspected dengue infection, tests to detect both dengue virus NS1 antigen and dengue IgM antibodies in human serum, plasma or whole blood OR a dengue polymerase chain reaction (PCR) test on whole blood or plasma should be requested.

rules.title: Dengue is a notifiable disease and should be reported by medical practitioners to the Communicable Diseases Ag
rules.message: Dengue is a notifiable disease and should be reported by medical practitioners to the Communicable Diseases Agency using the appropriate platform when suspected and when confirmed through point-of-care tests.

rules.title: Diagnostic testing for dengue should be considered in a pregnant patient early in the presentation, even in th
rules.message: Diagnostic testing for dengue should be considered in a pregnant patient early in the presentation, even in the absence of at least 3 cardinal symptoms (i.e.

rules.title: A full blood count (FBC) should be performed at the first opportunity when dengue is diagnosed or suspected
rules.message: A full blood count (FBC) should be performed at the first opportunity when dengue is diagnosed or suspected.

rules.title: Avoid nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) and intramuscular injections
rules.message: Avoid nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) and intramuscular injections.

rules.title: ■ Close monitoring should last 24–48 hours minimally until patient has entered into recovery phase
rules.message: ■ Close monitoring should last 24–48 hours minimally until patient has entered into recovery phase.

rules.title: Plan antiresorptive transition when stopping denosumab
rules.message: Stopping denosumab requires a planned transition, usually to a bisphosphonate, to reduce rebound bone loss and vertebral fracture risk.

rules.title: Diagnose osteoporosis with fragility fracture or DXA T-score at or below -2.5
rules.message: Osteoporosis is diagnosed by a current or previous fragility fracture, or a central DXA hip or spine T-score of -2.5 or lower.

rules.title: Use an oral bisphosphonate first-line when osteoporosis treatment is indicated
rules.message: When pharmacological osteoporosis treatment is indicated and suitable, oral alendronate or risedronate is preferred first-line; zoledronate or denosumab may suit selected patients.

rules.title: b Refer to supporting text under Recommendation 3a for common secondary causes of osteoporosis, including use
rules.message: b Refer to supporting text under Recommendation 3a for common secondary causes of osteoporosis, including use of certain medications.

rules.title: Menopausal hormone therapy (e
rules.message: Menopausal hormone therapy (e.g. combined oestrogen-progestogen regimen or tibolone) may be considered in selected cases, such as younger postmenopausal women, or those with early menopause or primary ovarian insufficiency. Consider referral to a specialist for further evaluation and management (see Recommendation 6).

rules.title: Any fracture occurring while on medication, including asymptomatic vertebral fractures, should prompt reassess
rules.message: Any fracture occurring while on medication, including asymptomatic vertebral fractures, should prompt reassessment of treatment strategy.

rules.title: If patient is assessed to be at very high risk of fractures, consider anabolic agents as first-line treatment,
rules.message: If patient is assessed to be at very high risk of fractures, consider anabolic agents as first-line treatment, with specialist input as needed.

rules.title: ‡ Possible osteoporosis risk should be explored in men younger than 65 years if they have significant risk fac
rules.message: ‡ Possible osteoporosis risk should be explored in men younger than 65 years if they have significant risk factors such as use of steroids or anti-androgens, or medical conditions associated with bone loss such as hypogonadism or hyperthyroidism.

rules.title: Postmenopausal women initially classified as low risk on OSTA, should be reassessed if they experience signifi
rules.message: Postmenopausal women initially classified as low risk on OSTA, should be reassessed if they experience significant weight loss, develop new clinical risk factors, or if their last assessment was five or more years ago.

rules.title: The 10-year probability of developing a fracture estimated by FRAX® should be interpreted in light of individu
rules.message: The 10-year probability of developing a fracture estimated by FRAX® should be interpreted in light of individual patient circumstances, as the parameters used by FRAX® in the calculation are not exhaustive.

rules.title: Commence post-stroke mobilisation within 48 hours unless contraindicated
rules.message: Stroke survivors should commence out-of-bed mobilisation within 48 hours unless contraindicated.

rules.title: Do not start intensive out-of-bed activity within 24 hours after stroke
rules.message: Starting intensive out-of-bed activity within 24 hours of stroke onset is not recommended.

rules.title: Start aphasia therapy within four weeks after stroke

required_any: within (?:the first )?(?:four|4) weeks|early aphasia therapy|speech and language therapy

rules.message: For stroke survivors with aphasia, provide early aphasia therapy starting within the first four weeks to maximise language recovery.

rules.title: b) Recommendation 83 was rephrased from “For stroke survivors with depression, non-invasive brain stimulation
rules.message: b) Recommendation 83 was rephrased from “For stroke survivors with depression, non-invasive brain stimulation (repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation [rTMS]) may be used.

rules.title: Non-pharmacological interventions addressing secondary stroke risk factors should be used for all people with
rules.message: Non-pharmacological interventions addressing secondary stroke risk factors should be used for all people with stroke and transient ischemic attack.

rules.title: For stroke survivors, rehabilitation should be structured to provide as much scheduled therapy (occupational t
rules.message: For stroke survivors, rehabilitation should be structured to provide as much scheduled therapy (occupational therapy and physiotherapy) as possible.

rules.title: For stroke survivors, group circuit class therapy should be used to increase scheduled therapy time
rules.message: For stroke survivors, group circuit class therapy should be used to increase scheduled therapy time.

rules.title: Where appropriate home-based coordinated stroke services are available, early supported discharge services sho
rules.message: Where appropriate home-based coordinated stroke services are available, early supported discharge services should be offered to stroke survivors with mild to moderate disability.

rules.title: Health professionals should initiate the process of setting goals, and involve stroke survivors and their fami
rules.message: Health professionals should initiate the process of setting goals, and involve stroke survivors and their families and carers throughout the process.

rules.title: Goals should be set in collaboration with the stroke survivor and their family/carer (unless they choose not t
rules.message: Goals should be set in collaboration with the stroke survivor and their family/carer (unless they choose not to participate) and should be well-defined, specific and challenging.

rules.title: Do not use raloxifene with active or previous venous thromboembolism

forbidden_any: raloxifene(?:.{0,30})(?:is suitable|is appropriate|is safe|can be used)

rules.message: Active or previous VTE is listed as a contraindication to raloxifene.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Do not use tibolone with active or previous venous thromboembolism

question_all: VTE|venous thrombo|deep vein thrombosis|\bDVT\b|pulmonary embol|\bPE\b|retinal vein thrombosis

rules.message: Tibolone is contraindicated in patients with active or previous venous thromboembolism.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Limit menopausal hormone therapy for osteoporosis to selected younger women

required_any: under 60|younger than 60|< ?60|within (?:ten|10) years (?:of|after) menopause|early menopause|vasomotor|hot flush

rules.message: Menopausal hormone therapy may be considered for osteoporosis prevention in selected women younger than 60 or within 10 years after menopause, particularly when vasomotor symptoms are present and contraindications are absent.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: a Information on contraindications and clinical considerations/precautions were sourced from HSA-approved Pack
rules.message: a Information on contraindications and clinical considerations/precautions were sourced from HSA-approved Package Inserts and UpToDate.5,6.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: c Menopausal hormone therapy, including tibolone and combined oestrogen-progestogen regimens, can be considere
rules.message: c Menopausal hormone therapy, including tibolone and combined oestrogen-progestogen regimens, can be considered for prevention of osteoporosis or fragility fractures in postmenopausal women <60 years old or within 10 years after menopause, or in women with early menopause (<45 years), unless contraindicated.8.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Tibolone is contraindicated with a history of angina, acute myocardial infarction, stroke, or transient ischae
rules.message: Tibolone is contraindicated with a history of angina, acute myocardial infarction, stroke, or transient ischaemic attack.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Tibolone is contraindicated with active endometrial cancer or endometrial hyperplasia
rules.message: Tibolone is contraindicated with active endometrial cancer or endometrial hyperplasia.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Tibolone is contraindicated with thrombophilic disorders such as protein C or protein S deficiency
rules.message: Tibolone is contraindicated with thrombophilic disorders such as protein C or protein S deficiency.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: There are additional pharmacological treatments for osteoporosis in postmenopausal women
rules.message: There are additional pharmacological treatments for osteoporosis in postmenopausal women.

source.document: Singapore Supplement on additional treatment options.pdf

rules.title: Avoid blind food elimination diets for atopic dermatitis
rules.message: Do not randomly eliminate food groups without a confirmed food-allergy diagnosis; this lacks proven benefit and can cause harm.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Avoid harsh or fragranced cleansers that trigger atopic dermatitis
rules.message: Minimise exposure to harsh surfactants, high-concentration ethanol, and fragrances when they irritate skin or trigger atopic dermatitis flares.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Avoid smoking and second-hand smoke in atopic dermatitis
rules.message: Avoid smoking and exposure to second-hand smoke because tobacco smoke can aggravate atopic dermatitis.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: While pet removal offers the most effective long-term control,22 households who choose to keep pets should foc
rules.message: While pet removal offers the most effective long-term control,22 households who choose to keep pets should focus on:22,23.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Avoid smoking and exposure to second-hand smoke21 (Refer to ‘Promoting smoking cessation and treating tobacco
rules.message: Avoid smoking and exposure to second-hand smoke21 (Refer to ‘Promoting smoking cessation and treating tobacco dependence’ ACG).

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Refer to an allergist or dermatologist for formal diagnosis of food allergy and provide counseling on appropri
rules.message: Refer to an allergist or dermatologist for formal diagnosis of food allergy and provide counseling on appropriate diet.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Avoid randomly or blindly eliminating food groups from patients’ diets, without confirmed diagnosis of food al
rules.message: Avoid randomly or blindly eliminating food groups from patients’ diets, without confirmed diagnosis of food allergy, as it is not proven to be effective in improving disease course and may cause nutritional deficiency, anxiety about food, higher food costs and promote IgE-mediated food allergy31,13,32.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Refer to the main ACG for the section on secondary infection of AD for more details
rules.message: Refer to the main ACG for the section on secondary infection of AD for more details.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Onset under the age of 2 years (do not use if children ≤4 years)
rules.message: Onset under the age of 2 years (do not use if children ≤4 years).

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Must fulfil at least 3 major criteria and 3 minor criteria
rules.message: Must fulfil at least 3 major criteria and 3 minor criteria.

source.document: Singapore Supplementary guide for AD ACG.pdf

rules.title: Avoid combining an ACE inhibitor with an ARB for hypertension
rules.message: The guideline advises avoiding ACE inhibitor plus ARB because of hyperkalaemia, acute kidney injury, and hypotension risk.

rules.title: Do not routinely start beta blockers as first-line hypertension monotherapy
rules.message: Do not initiate a beta blocker as first-line monotherapy for blood-pressure control unless a comorbidity provides a specific indication.

rules.title: Do not lower treated blood pressure below 120 over 70
rules.message: Do not lower treated blood pressure below 120/70 mmHg because benefit is inconsistent and adverse effects may increase.

rules.title: Use an ACE inhibitor, ARB, or CCB as first-line antihypertensive medications
rules.message: Use an ACE inhibitor, ARB, or CCB as first-line antihypertensive medications; consider thiazide/thiazide-like diuretics as alternative first-line if indicated.

rules.title: Avoid initiating beta blockers (BBs) as first-line monotherapy for BP control unless BB use is expected to hav
rules.message: Avoid initiating beta blockers (BBs) as first-line monotherapy for BP control unless BB use is expected to have favourable effect on patient comorbidities.

rules.title: Secondary hypertension refers to hypertension due to an underlying, identifiable cause
rules.message: Secondary hypertension refers to hypertension due to an underlying, identifiable cause.9 Suspicion of secondary hypertension should be higher in certain circumstances, such as early-onset, severe, or resistant hypertension.

rules.title: Thiazide/thiazide-like diuretics are considered as an alternative first-line as their side-effect profile is l
rules.message: Thiazide/thiazide-like diuretics are considered as an alternative first-line as their side-effect profile is less favourable compared to the other first-line antihypertensives.

rules.title: Table 2
rules.message: Table 2. Considerations for choosing a first-line antihypertensive class (adapted from product information leaflets; see Supplementary Table S1 for further details on specific agents).

rules.title: Preferred for patients who require treatment for migraine prevention, essential tremor, or portal hypertension
rules.message: Preferred for patients who require treatment for migraine prevention, essential tremor, or portal hypertension in cirrhosis (data supports the use of carvedilol also for heart failure).

rules.title: Consider metformin first-line for uncomplicated type 2 diabetes
rules.message: Metformin is the guideline's first-line medication consideration for most patients with T2DM, alongside lifestyle intervention.

rules.title: Consider SGLT2 inhibitor or GLP-1 receptor agonist for cardiorenal risk
rules.message: For T2DM with a need to reduce adverse cardiorenal outcomes, consider an SGLT2 inhibitor or GLP-1 receptor agonist based on patient factors.

rules.title: Regularly review treatment response and complications in T2DM
rules.message: Review all patients with T2DM regularly, including treatment response, glycaemic control, medication issues, and screening for complications.

rules.title: Target HbA1c should be individualised based on the patient’s overall health status, in consultation with the p
rules.message: Target HbA1c should be individualised based on the patient’s overall health status, in consultation with the patient.

rules.title: Safety (contraindications, adverse effects, risk of hypoglycaemia, precautions or monitoring)
rules.message: Safety (contraindications, adverse effects, risk of hypoglycaemia, precautions or monitoring).

rules.title: Metformin immediate-release formulations may require several doses per day
rules.message: Metformin immediate-release formulations may require several doses per day; this patient may prefer to use an extended-release once-daily formulation (at commencement, or after a short stabilisation and titration period with the immediate-release formulation).

rules.title: *These scenarios draw on some of the factors highlighted in Figure 1
rules.message: *These scenarios draw on some of the factors highlighted in Figure 1; these factors were chosen as examples and do not include all possible considerations.

rules.title: In patients with contraindication or intolerance to metformin [e
rules.message: In patients with contraindication or intolerance to metformin [e.g., if estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) <30 mL/min/1.73m2], the choice of first-line T2DM medication should follow the considerations outlined in Figure 1 on page 3.30.

rules.title: In addition to considering their cardiorenal benefits, the decision on the most appropriate medication for an
rules.message: In addition to considering their cardiorenal benefits, the decision on the most appropriate medication for an individual patient must include consideration of the factors mentioned in Figure 1 on page 3.

rules.title: ^d Further information can be found in the medication table, Non-insulin type 2 diabetes medications in Singap
rules.message: ^d Further information can be found in the medication table, Non-insulin type 2 diabetes medications in Singapore (see Useful resources on page 12); refer to individual product inserts for full details, including side effects of individual medications.

rules.title: Do not use SABA alone for long-term asthma treatment from age six
rules.message: For patients aged six years and older, SABA should not be used alone without a preventer for long-term asthma treatment.

rules.title: Include inhaled corticosteroid in long-term asthma management
rules.message: An inhaled corticosteroid is the mainstay of long-term asthma management because it treats airway inflammation and reduces exacerbations and mortality.

rules.title: Provide asthma self-management education and an action plan

required_any: action plan|written plan|inhaler technique|recognise.{0,20}worsen|when to seek|self[- ]management

rules.message: Educate patients and caregivers on asthma self-management, including correct inhaler use, recognising worsening symptoms, and following a written asthma action plan.

rules.title: Consequently, symptoms as well as factors known to influence symptoms or risk of poor outcomes (influencing fa
rules.message: Consequently, symptoms as well as factors known to influence symptoms or risk of poor outcomes (influencing factors) should be assessed.

rules.title: * Exacerbations refer to worsening of asthma symptoms beyond those typically experienced by the patient, somet
rules.message: * Exacerbations refer to worsening of asthma symptoms beyond those typically experienced by the patient, sometimes requiring urgent actions, including unscheduled clinic visits, use of oral corticosteroid, emergency department visits, and hospital admissions.

rules.title: Use the lowest effective ICS dose
rules.message: Use the lowest effective ICS dose. If treatment needs to be increased, consider adding another agent, such as a long-acting beta2 agonist (LABA) or a leukotriene receptor antagonist (LTRA), rather than increasing the ICS dose. If not possible to avoid long-term daily high-dose ICS, monitor patients closely for adverse effects and consider specialist referral.

rules.title: ^a Please refer to the Health Sciences Authority (HSA) Drug Safety Information No
rules.message: ^a Please refer to the Health Sciences Authority (HSA) Drug Safety Information No.

rules.title: Across the asthma treatment steps overall (see Figure 2 on page 6), daily ICS-containing treatment is the most
rules.message: Across the asthma treatment steps overall (see Figure 2 on page 6), daily ICS-containing treatment is the most effective preventer option at preventing or minimising asthma symptoms, and has the most evidence available—including long-term benefits on exacerbations and mortality.⁴⁴ Daily ICS-containing treatment is particularly important for patients at higher risk of poor asthma outcomes (for example, those with frequent or intense symptoms, or those with multiple BREATHE factors, as described.

rules.title: Add an SGLT2 inhibitor for CKD with persistent albuminuria
rules.message: For CKD with persistent albuminuria, the guideline recommends adding an SGLT2 inhibitor to ACE inhibitor or ARB therapy regardless of diabetes status.

rules.title: Use an ACE inhibitor or ARB for CKD with albuminuria
rules.message: Optimise blood pressure and albuminuria management in CKD with an ACE inhibitor or ARB, titrated as tolerated.

rules.title: Avoid NSAIDs in chronic kidney disease
rules.message: Avoid NSAIDs in CKD and review over-the-counter medicines for nephrotoxic ingredients; use safer alternatives where possible.

rules.title: Optimisation of lipid profile should be one of the key management goals for patients with CKD to reduce the ri
rules.message: Optimisation of lipid profile should be one of the key management goals for patients with CKD to reduce the risk of CV complications.

rules.title: In addition to optimising medications based on eGFR, possible side effects (for example, increased risk of myo
rules.message: In addition to optimising medications based on eGFR, possible side effects (for example, increased risk of myopathy/myalgia with statins in patients with more advanced CKD), cost and management of potential drug-drug interactions should be taken into account when deciding on lipid-lowering therapy in patients with CKD.

rules.title: See Supplementary Table 2: Commonly prescribed medications that should be used with caution (e
rules.message: See Supplementary Table 2: Commonly prescribed medications that should be used with caution (e.g.

rules.title: Lifestyle changes serve as an important complement to pharmacotherapy in patients with CKD, supporting prevent
rules.message: Lifestyle changes serve as an important complement to pharmacotherapy in patients with CKD, supporting prevention of disease progression and complications, as well as enhancing the quality of life among patients with CKD.35 Lifestyle intervention needs to be tailored to the individual (considering factors such as comorbidities, age, and social context) and patients should be involved when setting achievable and sustainable goals.

rules.title: However, for patients on ACE inhibitor/ARB with hyperkalaemia, reducing potassium intake through diet may be r
rules.message: However, for patients on ACE inhibitor/ARB with hyperkalaemia, reducing potassium intake through diet may be required and it is usually preferred over stopping pharmacotherapy.

rules.title: Unless contraindicated, sodium restriction can be considered in order to reduce blood pressure39, albuminuria3
rules.message: Unless contraindicated, sodium restriction can be considered in order to reduce blood pressure39, albuminuria39, and risk of CV events.40.

rules.title: Selection of parameters for review should be tailored to the patients’ CKD stage and clinical needs, such as c
rules.message: Selection of parameters for review should be tailored to the patients’ CKD stage and clinical needs, such as comorbidities management.

rules.title: Use UACR to assess albuminuria for early CKD detection
rules.message: UACR is preferred over UPCR for detecting early progressive kidney damage.

rules.title: Require at least three months of abnormality for CKD diagnosis
rules.message: CKD diagnosis requires reduced GFR or a marker of kidney damage to be present for at least three months.

rules.title: Monitor people at increased CKD risk at least annually
rules.message: People at increased risk who do not currently have CKD should still be monitored for CKD at least annually, with closer follow-up when risk factors are poorly controlled.

rules.title: For patients who do not have CKD but are at increased risk, monitor for CKD regularly
rules.message: For patients who do not have CKD but are at increased risk, monitor for CKD regularly.

rules.title: ** Findings present for at least three months which meet CKD diagnostic criteria (see Recommendation 2 on page
rules.message: ** Findings present for at least three months which meet CKD diagnostic criteria (see Recommendation 2 on page 3).

rules.title: For ease of clinical application and in line with international consensus, this clinical guidance adopts a com
rules.message: For ease of clinical application and in line with international consensus, this clinical guidance adopts a common albuminuria threshold of UACR ≥3 mg/mmol (≥30 mg/g) for both males and females,¹ while acknowledging that gender-specific thresholds may still be used in some practice settings in Singapore.

rules.title: During routine health screening, such as annual medical examinations, a urine dipstick test may detect protein
rules.message: During routine health screening, such as annual medical examinations, a urine dipstick test may detect proteinuria or haematuria.

rules.title: The cause of disease is included as part of CKD staging as it is fundamentally important in prognostication an
rules.message: The cause of disease is included as part of CKD staging as it is fundamentally important in prognostication and choice of cause-specific treatments.

rules.title: Escalate acute severe diabetic foot conditions immediately

question_any: severe infection|wet gangrene|acute limb ischaemia|acute limb ischemia|acute Charcot|red hot swollen foot

rules.message: Severe infection, wet gangrene, acute Charcot neuro-osteoarthropathy, or acute limb ischaemia requires immediate emergency-department referral.

source.recommendation: Active diabetic foot conditions referral pathway

rules.title: Advise patients at diabetic foot risk not to walk barefoot
rules.message: Patients with diabetes should avoid walking barefoot or in socks without shoes, including at home, to reduce foot injury and ulcer risk.

rules.title: Use diabetic foot risk findings to set review frequency

required_any: risk category|risk level|more frequent|at least annual|yearly|neuropathy|peripheral arterial|previous ulcer|previous amputation

rules.message: Use diabetic foot assessment findings to classify ulcer risk, determine review frequency, and identify the need for referral.

rules.title: Foot assessment for patients with diabetes mellitus begins with checking for presence of active diabetic foot
rules.message: Foot assessment for patients with diabetes mellitus begins with checking for presence of active diabetic foot conditions, such as infection, acute Charcot neuro-osteoarthropathy, or limb ischaemia.

rules.title: Treat the patient immediately and refer for timely management by multidisciplinary diabetic foot clinic or spe
rules.message: Treat the patient immediately and refer for timely management by multidisciplinary diabetic foot clinic or specialist (e.g.

rules.title: Foot assessment is only one component of diabetes management and its associated conditions (refer to type 2 di
rules.message: Foot assessment is only one component of diabetes management and its associated conditions (refer to type 2 diabetes mellitus management ACG).

rules.title: Referral to specialists, podiatrists, or other healthcare professionals may be required based on findings from
rules.message: Referral to specialists, podiatrists, or other healthcare professionals may be required based on findings from foot assessment.

rules.title: ABPI >0
rules.message: ABPI >0.9 in patients with diabetes mellitus does not exclude PAD and should be interpreted carefully, as it could be a false elevation due to vascular calcification.17.

rules.title: With the tip of index finger, lightly sequentially touch the tips of the first, third, and fifth toes of both
rules.message: With the tip of index finger, lightly sequentially touch the tips of the first, third, and fifth toes of both feet for 1–2s (do not push, tap or poke).

rules.title: Avoid walking barefoot or in socks without shoes, even at home
rules.message: Avoid walking barefoot or in socks without shoes, even at home; wear home sandals with non-slip soles.

rules.title: Do not use HbA1c to screen for or diagnose diabetes during pregnancy
rules.message: HbA1c should not be used to screen for or diagnose diabetes during pregnancy.

source.recommendation: Avoiding HbA1c for screening and diagnosis during pregnancy

rules.title: Use a three-point OGTT to screen for GDM at 24 to 28 weeks
rules.message: At 24 to 28 weeks, screen women without known diabetes or pre-diabetes using a three-point 75 g OGTT.

rules.title: Perform postpartum OGTT after diabetes diagnosed during pregnancy

question_relaxed_any: (?=.*(?:gestational diabetes|\bGDM\b|diabetes during pregnancy))(?=.*(?:after delivery|postpartum|after birth|had my baby|future diabetes|later diabetes|follow[- ]?up))

rules.message: At 6 to 12 weeks after delivery, reassess glycaemic status using a two-point 75 g OGTT after diabetes was diagnosed during pregnancy.

rules.title: Individual patient circumstances should also be taken into consideration when evaluating the need to test with
rules.message: Individual patient circumstances should also be taken into consideration when evaluating the need to test with fasting plasma glucose (FPG)/2-point 75 g OGTT.

rules.title: Women with diabetes diagnosed during pregnancy who are found to have normal glycaemic status at 6 to 12 weeks
rules.message: Women with diabetes diagnosed during pregnancy who are found to have normal glycaemic status at 6 to 12 weeks after delivery should be regularly screened for diabetes every 1 to 3 years (ideally annually) from then on.1.

rules.title: All women with diabetes diagnosed during pregnancy should receive timely management, including adopting a heal

question_relaxed_any: (?=.*(?:I (?:have|just found out|was diagnosed with) gestational diabetes|my gestational diabetes|diabetes during my pregnancy))(?=.*(?:what should I do|doing differently|manage|management|baby|pregnancy|concerned|worried))

rules.message: All women with diabetes diagnosed during pregnancy should receive timely management, including adopting a healthy lifestyle.

rules.title: Start lifestyle intervention and glucose monitoring (refer to the ACG “Managing pre-diabetes – a growing healt
rules.message: Start lifestyle intervention and glucose monitoring (refer to the ACG “Managing pre-diabetes – a growing health concern” as appropriate).

rules.title: ‡Results should be confirmed by repeating the test with the result above the diagnostic threshold
rules.message: ‡Results should be confirmed by repeating the test with the result above the diagnostic threshold.

rules.title: Women with a history of GDM are about 10 times more likely to develop type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) than wom
rules.message: Women with a history of GDM are about 10 times more likely to develop type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) than women who had a normoglycaemic pregnancy.7 In Singapore, it is estimated that 4 in 10 women with a history of GDM develop T2DM or pre-diabetes within 4 to 6 years from delivery.21,22 Among women with a history of GDM, more frequent follow-up may be required for those who received insulin during pregnancy or those with other risk factors for developing diabetes such as non-pregnancy BMI ≥23.

rules.title: Do not stop urate-lowering therapy during an acute gout flare
rules.message: The guideline says to continue urate-lowering therapy during an acute flare.

rules.title: Provide flare prophylaxis when initiating urate-lowering therapy
rules.message: When ULT is initiated, provide flare prophylaxis, usually colchicine; alternatives are a low-dose NSAID or corticosteroid when appropriate.

rules.title: Warn patients starting allopurinol or febuxostat about severe skin reactions
rules.message: A new rash or systemic symptoms after starting allopurinol or febuxostat may indicate a severe cutaneous adverse reaction and requires stopping the drug and urgent medical assessment.

rules.title: Contraindicated in patients with previous hypersensitivity to allopurinol (including SCARs)
rules.message: Contraindicated in patients with previous hypersensitivity to allopurinol (including SCARs); consider other ULT options.

rules.title: Initiate ULT for patients with gout who meet the ULT treatment criteria, starting at a low dose and slowly tit
rules.message: Initiate ULT for patients with gout who meet the ULT treatment criteria, starting at a low dose and slowly titrating upwards as needed (see Recommendation 1).

rules.title: Mitigate the risk of developing SCARs and provide counselling on SCAR monitoring for patients on allopurinol o
rules.message: Mitigate the risk of developing SCARs and provide counselling on SCAR monitoring for patients on allopurinol or febuxostat (see Recommendation 3).

rules.title: Ensure prophylaxis against acute flares during initial period of ULT (see Recommendation 2)
rules.message: Ensure prophylaxis against acute flares during initial period of ULT (see Recommendation 2).

rules.title: Manage acute flares as soon as possible, including treatment with colchicine, NSAIDs, or corticosteroids (see
rules.message: Manage acute flares as soon as possible, including treatment with colchicine, NSAIDs, or corticosteroids (see Recommendation 4).

rules.title: Review concomitant diabetes medicines when starting basal insulin
rules.message: Concomitant T2DM medicines should be reviewed when basal insulin starts and continued at the current or lower dose where appropriate.

rules.title: Do not give oral glucose to an unconscious hypoglycaemic patient
rules.message: Do not give anything orally to an unconscious or unresponsive person with hypoglycaemia because of choking risk; emergency assistance is required.

rules.title: Start basal insulin when targets remain unmet on optimal non-insulin therapy

question_any: not controlled|above target|high HbA1c|medicines not working|despite treatment|when|blood sugar|diet|exercise|daily routine|side effects|what .*entail|overwhelming

required_any: not met|above target|despite|optimal|non[- ]insulin|other diabetes medicines|current treatment

rules.message: Start basal insulin when glycaemic targets are not met despite optimal treatment with non-insulin T2DM medicines.

rules.title: Different insulin compounds (Table 1), even within the same time-action category, are not identical and the de
rules.message: Different insulin compounds (Table 1), even within the same time-action category, are not identical and the decision to switch between products should be evaluated by the clinician.

rules.title: Patients with HU do not experience or perceive typical early warning symptoms of hypoglycaemia (such as tremor
rules.message: Patients with HU do not experience or perceive typical early warning symptoms of hypoglycaemia (such as tremors, palpitations, sweating) when their blood glucose is low.

rules.title: Advise patients with HU to raise their glycaemic targets for several weeks to months to avoid hypoglycaemia
rules.message: Advise patients with HU to raise their glycaemic targets for several weeks to months to avoid hypoglycaemia.28-30 Use of continuous glucose monitoring could be helpful.

rules.title: ○ Insulin should be injected subcutaneously into the abdomen, arms, thighs, or buttocks
rules.message: ○ Insulin should be injected subcutaneously into the abdomen, arms, thighs, or buttocks.

rules.title: ○ Patients should avoid areas with bruises, scar tissue, parts near joints, the groin, and the navel
rules.message: ○ Patients should avoid areas with bruises, scar tissue, parts near joints, the groin, and the navel.

rules.title: Used syringes and needles should be discarded in a puncture-resistant container (hard plastic/ metal/sharps co
rules.message: Used syringes and needles should be discarded in a puncture-resistant container (hard plastic/ metal/sharps container) with a secured lid.

rules.title: Remind patients to rotate injection sites regularly within their preferred body area to avoid the development
rules.message: Remind patients to rotate injection sites regularly within their preferred body area to avoid the development of lipohypertrophy.

rules.title: Include lifestyle intervention in pre-diabetes management
rules.message: Lifestyle intervention should be recommended to all people with pre-diabetes.

rules.title: Do not diagnose pre-diabetes using HbA1c alone
rules.message: The guideline does not indicate HbA1c as a diagnostic test for pre-diabetes; use fasting plasma glucose and/or a two-hour OGTT threshold.

rules.title: Reserve metformin for selected pre-diabetes after lifestyle intervention

required_any: lifestyle|diet|exercise|does not improve|unable to adopt|after a trial

rules.message: Lifestyle intervention remains first-line for pre-diabetes; consider metformin when glycaemia does not improve despite lifestyle intervention or the person cannot adopt it, especially with additional risk factors.

rules.title: Avoid pastries, fried food, and food containing coconut milk or cream
rules.message: Avoid pastries, fried food, and food containing coconut milk or cream.

rules.title: Start metformin at 250 mg twice daily and gradually increase as tolerated if required (depending on glycaemic
rules.message: Start metformin at 250 mg twice daily and gradually increase as tolerated if required (depending on glycaemic status), up to 850 mg twice daily.

rules.title: Engage in exercises that require intensity and that accelerate the heart rate
rules.message: Engage in exercises that require intensity and that accelerate the heart rate.

rules.title: Use warfarin rather than a DOAC for AF with mechanical valves or significant mitral stenosis
rules.message: Warfarin is the treatment of choice for AF with a mechanical heart valve or moderate-to-severe mitral stenosis; DOACs are excluded.

rules.title: Do not use antiplatelet therapy for AF-related stroke prevention
rules.message: Antiplatelet medicines are generally not recommended for preventing atrial-fibrillation-related stroke; assess the need for oral anticoagulation instead.

rules.title: Start anticoagulation for AF stroke score of two or more
rules.message: For AF with a modified CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more, oral anticoagulation is indicated unless contraindicated.

rules.title: When mCHA2DS2VASc score = 1, the decision to start OAC should consider patient-specific factors such as age or
rules.message: When mCHA2DS2VASc score = 1, the decision to start OAC should consider patient-specific factors such as age or underlying conditions.

rules.title: Routine monitoring with coagulation tests is not necessary or useful in patients on DOACs, except in cases of
rules.message: Routine monitoring with coagulation tests is not necessary or useful in patients on DOACs, except in cases of severe bleeding or urgent surgery.

rules.title: *** DOACs are contraindicated in patients with Child-Pugh C liver cirrhosis
rules.message: *** DOACs are contraindicated in patients with Child-Pugh C liver cirrhosis; rivaroxaban is also contraindicated in patients with Child-Pugh B liver cirrhosis.

rules.title: ††† See Table 2 for more details
rules.message: ††† See Table 2 for more details. Patients taking systemic strong CYP3A4 or P-gp inhibitors may be contraindicated for DOAC use. Please consult a pharmacist or appropriate online resources^41 for information on medications with interacting metabolic pathways, especially when patients are taking new concomitant medications or supplements.

rules.title: Conduct monitoring tests to ensure the safe use of OAC therapy (see Recommendation 3)
rules.message: Conduct monitoring tests to ensure the safe use of OAC therapy (see Recommendation 3).

rules.title: No anticoagulants
rules.message: No anticoagulants. Reassess stroke risk at least annually, and when clinical circumstances change. (see Recommendation 4).

rules.title: Start anticoagulation therapy
rules.message: Start anticoagulation therapy. Consider a DOAC over warfarin, based on patient, medication and disease factors. (see Recommendation 2).

rules.title: Do not routinely treat asymptomatic bacteriuria in non-pregnant adults
rules.message: Do not routinely screen for or treat asymptomatic bacteriuria within the guideline's healthy, non-pregnant adult scope.

rules.title: Do not use nitrofurantoin for acute pyelonephritis
rules.message: Nitrofurantoin and fosfomycin do not reach therapeutic renal-tissue concentrations and should not be used for acute pyelonephritis.

rules.title: Obtain urine testing and culture for suspected pyelonephritis
rules.message: For suspected uncomplicated pyelonephritis, obtain urine dipstick or microscopy plus culture and sensitivity to confirm diagnosis and guide treatment.

rules.title: While most cases of uncomplicated cystitis are self-limiting and will resolve with appropriate pharmacological
rules.message: While most cases of uncomplicated cystitis are self-limiting and will resolve with appropriate pharmacological and non-pharmacological treatment, there may be instances when a referral to a specialist may be needed (see Considerations for referral under Recommendation 7).

rules.title: In patients with acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis, start empirical treatment with oral antibiotics as soon a
rules.message: In patients with acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis, start empirical treatment with oral antibiotics as soon as possible after conducting a urine C&S (see Recommendation 5), and tailor treatment based on C&S results.8 Antibiotics that do not reach therapeutic concentrations in renal tissue (such as nitrofurantoin and fosfomycin) play a limited role in the management of acute uncomplicated pyelonephritis and should not be used.8,10.

rules.title: Nitrofurantoin may not be a suitable treatment option for men with suspected prostate involvement (i
rules.message: Nitrofurantoin may not be a suitable treatment option for men with suspected prostate involvement (i.e.

rules.title: Screening and treatment for asymptomatic bacteriuria in healthy, non-pregnant women should not be routinely co
rules.message: Screening and treatment for asymptomatic bacteriuria in healthy, non-pregnant women should not be routinely conducted.

rules.title: The test should be conducted prior to commencing treatment, but empirical antibiotic treatment should be start
rules.message: The test should be conducted prior to commencing treatment, but empirical antibiotic treatment should be started as soon as possible without waiting for C&S results.

rules.title: **Amoxicillin-clavulanate is an option** for patients for whom nitrofurantoin is unsuitable or contraindicated
rules.message: **Amoxicillin-clavulanate is an option** for patients for whom nitrofurantoin is unsuitable or contraindicated.

rules.title: 2) Alternative to antibiotic therapy – this may be an option for patients with well-tolerated mild to moderate
rules.message: 2) Alternative to antibiotic therapy – this may be an option for patients with well-tolerated mild to moderate symptoms who would like to avoid antibiotic use.

rules.title: Start anticoagulation promptly for confirmed proximal DVT or pulmonary embolism
rules.message: Confirmed proximal DVT or PE requires anticoagulation as soon as possible unless contraindicated.

rules.title: Prefer LMWH for cancer-associated VTE with gastrointestinal cancer

question_all: gastrointestinal cancer|GI cancer|stomach cancer|bowel cancer|colorectal cancer|oesophageal cancer|esophageal cancer

rules.message: For cancer-associated VTE, LMWH is preferred when the patient has gastrointestinal cancer.

rules.title: Treat general-population VTE with a DOAC for at least three months
rules.message: For most patients with VTE in the general population, use a DOAC as preferred therapy for at least three months, with duration individualised thereafter.

rules.title: ‡ DOAC use may be contraindicated in patients taking systemic strong CYP3A4 or P-glycoprotein inhibitors
rules.message: ‡ DOAC use may be contraindicated in patients taking systemic strong CYP3A4 or P-glycoprotein inhibitors.

rules.title: LMWH monotherapy can be considered for these patients
rules.message: LMWH monotherapy can be considered for these patients.33 Although DOACs such as apixaban, edoxaban, and rivaroxaban can be used for patients with Child Pugh-A cirrhosis, they are contraindicated for patients with liver disease and coagulopathy.

rules.title: All patients on extended treatment, including those with cancer, should be reviewed at least once a year and w
rules.message: All patients on extended treatment, including those with cancer, should be reviewed at least once a year and when clinically indicated to assess for any change that may necessitate adjustments in the management (such as the choice or dose of the medication).5,7,9.

rules.title: VTE treatment for patients who are in remission is similar to that for the general population (see Recommendat
rules.message: VTE treatment for patients who are in remission is similar to that for the general population (see Recommendation 2), with the risk of VTE becoming comparable to that of patients without cancer after two years of remission.46 While evidence on treatment duration for these patients is lacking, three to six months is an appropriate starting point for decision-making, to be tailored to the patient’s individual circumstances.

rules.title: The risk of thrombus extension is highest in the first few days after VTE is diagnosed
rules.message: The risk of thrombus extension is highest in the first few days after VTE is diagnosed.4 Therefore, starting anticoagulation as soon as possible is crucial to prevent extension, VTE recurrence, morbidity, and death.

rules.title: As renal impairment increases the risk of VTE and bleeding events,31 patients with reduced renal function may
rules.message: As renal impairment increases the risk of VTE and bleeding events,31 patients with reduced renal function may require dose adjustments (see Table 1) or more frequent monitoring.

rules.title: Escalate thunderclap headache for urgent CT assessment
rules.message: A thunderclap headache requires urgent CT brain assessment for suspected intracranial bleeding.

rules.title: Do not routinely image uncomplicated migraine or tension-type headache
rules.message: CT and MRI are not indicated for migraine or tension-type headache without red flags.

rules.title: Use CT for headache developing within seven days after head trauma
rules.message: A headache developing within seven days after head trauma warrants CT brain assessment.

rules.title: Considerations informing the choice between CT and MRI include clinical presentation, contraindications, diagn
rules.message: Considerations informing the choice between CT and MRI include clinical presentation, contraindications, diagnostic accuracy, and availability of the test.

rules.title: Patients with thunderclap headache (TCH), defined as severe headache of sudden onset that reaches maximum inte
rules.message: Patients with thunderclap headache (TCH), defined as severe headache of sudden onset that reaches maximum intensity within a minute, require urgent CT brain to investigate suspected intracranial bleeding, particularly subarachnoid haemorrhage (SAH).

rules.title: Suspicion for brain cancer or central nervous system infection should be higher when a patient presents with a
rules.message: Suspicion for brain cancer or central nervous system infection should be higher when a patient presents with associated features, including systemic symptoms or signs (such as fever, chills, night sweat, myalgia, or weight loss), history of cancer, and immunosuppression.

rules.title: Escalate possible cauda equina syndrome urgently
rules.message: Possible cauda equina features require urgent assessment; the guideline states that MRI is indicated.

rules.title: Do not routinely order MRI for initial non-specific low back pain
rules.message: MRI is not indicated for initial non-specific low back pain, even with radicular symptoms, unless a specific pathology or red flag is suspected.

rules.title: Order MRI for progressive neurological deficits with low back pain
rules.message: Low back pain with progressive neurological symptoms or signs requires MRI to identify the underlying spinal pathology, especially when progression is rapid.

rules.title: Decision-making regarding further investigations, including imaging, should take into account whether the susp
rules.message: Decision-making regarding further investigations, including imaging, should take into account whether the suspected spinal pathology would require urgent or specialised management (such as surgery), and potential consequences of missing or delaying the diagnosis.

rules.title: One should suspect cancer or infection of the spine when a patient with low back pain presents with associated
rules.message: One should suspect cancer or infection of the spine when a patient with low back pain presents with associated features such as fever or chills, unexplained weight loss, history of cancer, immunosuppression, pain at rest or at night, intravenous drug use, or bacteraemia.

rules.title: The largest group of patients with low back pain (more than 80%) have non-specific low back pain
rules.message: The largest group of patients with low back pain (more than 80%) have non-specific low back pain.⁴ Typically, a diagnosis of non-specific low back pain is reached when a precise source or defined pathoanatomical cause of pain cannot be identified.

rules.title: Conservative management of low back pain encompasses various strategies, such as use of nonsteroidal anti-infl
rules.message: Conservative management of low back pain encompasses various strategies, such as use of nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), exercise, or manual therapy.

rules.title: Do not recommend chest X-ray for lung cancer screening
rules.message: CXR is not indicated for lung cancer screening; the guideline identifies low-dose CT as the screening test for higher-risk individuals.

rules.title: Do not routinely order preoperative chest X-rays without clinical indications
rules.message: Routine preoperative CXR is generally not indicated when history, examination, and symptoms do not suggest a clinical need.

rules.title: Do not routinely use chest X-ray for a general medical examination

question_all: medical examination|medical check[- ]?up|employment medical|school admission|routine physical

rules.message: Routine CXR as part of a general medical examination usually has low diagnostic yield and may not be indicated without a specific clinical reason.

rules.title: Individuals should be advised to check and adhere to prevailing medical examination requirements stipulated by
rules.message: Individuals should be advised to check and adhere to prevailing medical examination requirements stipulated by the employer, admitting higher learning institution, or other relevant agencies (for example, Ministry of Manpower or Ministry of Education).

rules.title: When there are no symptoms and no significant findings from history and physical examination suggesting that C
rules.message: When there are no symptoms and no significant findings from history and physical examination suggesting that CXR may be required, CXR findings usually do not alter the management decision or clinical outcomes.1,10 CXR would still be useful for specific clinical presentations, with some examples below of targeted use of CXR for preoperative assessment.
